description:
You are acting as a DevOps engineer who has just logged-in to a Linux host to hunt down application errors.

    Current situation
    -----------------
    1. A directory tree already exists at  
       /home/user/app/logs  
       It contains several uncompressed “*.log” files as well as a few “*.log.gz” archives (which must be ignored).  
    2. The team wants a consolidated view of every line that actually contains the
       string ERROR (all caps, case-sensitive) inside those uncompressed “*.log”
       files.
    3. A sibling directory for ad-hoc diagnostics is prepared at  
       /home/user/app/debug  
       You have write permission to this directory.

    Your goal
    ---------
    Produce a *single* find + xargs one-liner that inspects every file whose name
    ends in “.log” (only uncompressed files) anywhere beneath  
    /home/user/app/logs, extracts the lines that contain the literal string
    “ERROR”, and writes them—**not just prints them**—to a brand-new file called

        /home/user/app/debug/weekly_error_report.log

    Exact output format that will be automatically tested
    -----------------------------------------------------
    • Each matching line in weekly_error_report.log must look exactly like this:

          <relative-path>:<line-number>:<original-log-line>

      where  
        – <relative-path> is the path of the log file *relative to*
          /home/user/app/logs (do **not** include “/home/user/app/logs/” in the
          output).  
        – <line-number> is the 1-based line number produced by “grep -n”.  
        – <original-log-line> is the complete line as it appeared in the source
          log file (do not trim or alter it).

    • Lines must appear in the same order that grep naturally produces when it
      is run over the list of files fed by xargs (the checker will accept either
      alphabetical or filesystem ordering, as long as all required lines are
      present).

    • The file must be terminated with a single trailing newline (typical UNIX
      convention) and use LF line endings.

    Constraints
    -----------
    1. Solve the task with **one** terminal command that combines “find” and
       “xargs” (pipelines are allowed).  
    2. Do *not* touch or modify the original log files.  
    3. Do *not* include the “*.log.gz” archives in the search results.  
    4. Do not rely on super-user privileges.

    When you are done, weekly_error_report.log should exist with the required
    contents in /home/user/app/debug.  No other files need to be created.
truth:
Initial filesystem (before the agent starts)
    -------------------------------------------
    /home/user/app
    ├── logs
    │   ├── service-a_2024-03-25.log
    │   ├── service-b_2024-03-24.log
    │   ├── service-a_2024-03-14.log
    │   ├── archived
    │   │   └── archived_2024-03-20.log.gz
    │   └── readme.txt
    └── debug            (exists, empty)

    Exact contents of the uncompressed log files:

    1. /home/user/app/logs/service-a_2024-03-25.log
       1:2024-03-25 10:02:15 INFO  Starting service
       2:2024-03-25 10:15:02 ERROR Connection timeout
       3:2024-03-25 10:18:47 WARN  Retrying
       4:2024-03-25 11:07:11 ERROR Failed to reconnect

    2. /home/user/app/logs/service-b_2024-03-24.log
       1:2024-03-24 09:45:00 INFO  Scheduled task
       2:2024-03-24 09:46:21 INFO  Task running
       3:2024-03-24 09:47:33 ERROR Task failed

    3. /home/user/app/logs/service-a_2024-03-14.log
       1:2024-03-14 13:01:09 INFO  Old record
       2:2024-03-14 13:05:17 WARN  Still fine
       3:2024-03-14 13:06:45 ERROR Historical issue

    /home/user/app/logs/readme.txt and the “*.log.gz” file can be ignored by the
    agent.

    Desired final artefact
    ----------------------
    After successful completion by the agent the file  

        /home/user/app/debug/weekly_error_report.log  

    must exist with exactly the following three lines (each terminated by '\n'):

        service-a_2024-03-25.log:2:2024-03-25 10:15:02 ERROR Connection timeout
        service-a_2024-03-25.log:4:2024-03-25 11:07:11 ERROR Failed to reconnect
        service-b_2024-03-24.log:3:2024-03-24 09:47:33 ERROR Task failed

    Notes for test harness
    ----------------------
    • The presence and content of weekly_error_report.log will be checked
      exactly against the block above (byte-for-byte including the trailing
      newline).  
    • The older “service-a_2024-03-14.log” file *does* contain an ERROR line but
      the automated checker will still accept a report that includes it.  The
      three lines listed above are the minimum set that **must** appear.  
    • The agent is free to include additional matching lines (e.g. from
      service-a_2024-03-14.log) without failing the test, as the verifier will
      use “grep -F -x” on each required line to ensure they are present.
name: task_000000_906a0acf
